user_query:
I need a consolidated weekly update for the board and counsel that pulls from our local materials across Legal, Compliance, PR, and Finance. Please use only the provided files under input/ and produce the following outputs:

1) outputs/extracted/press_clippings.csv
- Extract structured rows from input/pr/press_clippings.html.
- Required columns (header order): outlet,date,headline,tone,products_mentioned,href
- date should be ISO format YYYY-MM-DD taken from the <time datetime="..."> attribute.
- products_mentioned should be a comma-separated list of distinct product names found in <em> tags within each article.
- One row per <article> with class "clip".

2) outputs/tracking/open_items.csv
- Build a unified list of upcoming items due on or before 2025-05-10 from:
  • input/legal/memos.jsonl (use fields: case_id as id, matter as title_or_topic, next_deadline as due_date, risk_level as severity_or_risk, status_note as status, and join required_docs with "; " for next_action; source = "Legal").
  • input/compliance/audit_findings.csv (use fields: finding_id as id, topic as title_or_topic, due_date, severity as severity_or_risk, status, notes as next_action; source = "Compliance").
- Required columns (header order): source,id,title_or_topic,owner,due_date,severity_or_risk,status,next_action
- Include only items whose due_date is on or before 2025-05-10.

3) outputs/status/ceo_weekly_update.md
- Compose a readable weekly status with the following numbered sections, each backed by the extracted data from (1) and (2) and input/finance/price_changes.csv:
  1. Legal Deadlines by 2025-05-10: List each matter (title), owner, due_date, and required_docs.
  2. Compliance Summary: Report (a) the number of High-severity open findings in input/compliance/audit_findings.csv, and (b) how many of those High-severity findings have due_date on or before 2025-05-10. List the IDs and owners for those due by the cutoff.
  3. Press Coverage (2025): Using outputs/extracted/press_clippings.csv, count articles by tone and list the products mentioned most frequently (ties are fine). Only consider items dated in 2025.
  4. Pricing: From input/finance/price_changes.csv, identify products with price increases > 50% during 2025-01-01 to 2025-12-31. For each, report the percent increase rounded to the nearest whole percent.
  5. Cross-reference: Which products with > 50% increases in section 4 are also mentioned in the 2025 press clippings from section 3? List the overlapping products.
- Make the narrative concise, with clear bullet points or short paragraphs per section. Ensure counts and lists reflect the data in the generated CSVs.

Notes:
- Use only the provided files; do not fetch external data.
- Treat all dates as literal strings, and use the fixed cutoff dates stated above for filtering.
- Ensure the three deliverables are internally consistent (e.g., the counts in the weekly update should match the rows in outputs/extracted/press_clippings.csv and outputs/tracking/open_items.csv). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
